message.content: Adversaries may acquire a botnet, a network of compromised systems, to facilitate coordinated malicious activities. This infrastructure can be bought, leased, or rented, often through booter/stresser services. Botnets enable adversaries to execute large-scale operations such as Phishing campaigns or Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks, significantly amplifying their impact. Common exploitation methods include leveraging the botnet for overwhelming network traffic or mass-distributing malicious emails. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring for unusual network traffic patterns, implementing robust email filtering, and employing DDoS protection services. Regularly updating and patching systems can also reduce the risk of devices being compromised and added to a botnet.
